name: Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 2/3 fresh cake yeast dissolved in 2/3 cup warm water
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 2 cups flour with the dissolved yeast. Knead the dough for 10 minutes, either by hand or in the bowl of an electric mixer with a dough hook.
2. Shape the dough into a ball and put it in an oiled bowl to rise until doubled in bulk, about 3 hours.
3. Punch down the dough. Turn it out onto a floured board and knead again, incorporating the remaining flour, 1/3 cup olive oil, water, and salt.
4. Knead until the dough is smooth and elastic. Let it rise again in a covered bowl for another 3 hours.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. Divide the dough into two equal pieces. Roll out each piece on a well-floured board to circles or squares that are about 1/2 inch thick.
7. Brush the rolled-out dough with the remaining olive oil and press toppings of your choice onto the top of each circle.
8. Bake on a baking sheet until golden brown, about 20 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature, cut into wedges, squares, or triangles.
NOTE: Slashes may be made in a decorative or random pattern in the breads before baking.
TOPPINGS:  Thinly sliced rounds of yellow onion  Paper-thin crosswise slices of tomatoes and chunks of sun-dried tomatoes  Oregano leaves and slivers of garlic  Coarse salt and olive oil  Rosemary sprigs, coarse salt, and olive oil  Black olive halves and coarse salt  Sage leaves, coarse salt, and olive oil
